text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy village deep in the forest, there was a mysterious old bookworm named Oakenleaf. Every afternoon after the sun went orange, he would snuggle inside a big old oak near the center of town. He knew nothing about Owlishowl OwOLS OwOLS OwOLS OwOLS dreams or Owlfunny owl things.

One autumn day a young girl named Luna found him. She was amazed by his feathers and listened longingly as he fluttered tales of faraway lands. Oakenleaf told her stories so vivid she could see each leaf change. They became fast friends.

Winter came, and Oakenleaf didn't fly. Instead, he read to Luna by the hearth. When spring came, he fluttered back into the oak.

That summer, Luna went on a picnic by the woods. She sat under an oak just like Oaken_leaf. She read aloud from their old favorite. Suddenly there was a rustle. They glanced up together.
